_comment: Ratchet baseline for the Rust coverage gate. Written ONLY by
the script refuses to write it from any other platform, because the gate is pinned to Linux/CI
and the workspace has 25 cfg(windows)/cfg(unix) sites that make the platforms non-comparable.
`crates` is EMPTY on purpose. The only figures measured so far came from a Windows host
(2026-07-21, HEAD ea75441, .orchestration/logs/t75-coverage.md) and baking those into a Linux
gate is exactly what the standing ruling forbids. Until the coverage job runs once on
ubuntu-latest and records real numbers here, every non-hard-90 crate reports as 'no baseline'
and is not gated. The four hard-90% crates are rule-driven and need no baseline.
